title: Teaching Kit: Present the Stack
summary: A ready-to-present deck outline. Each slide has talking points and a one-line script you can read aloud. Move top to bottom and you will have taught the entire Foundry + MCP + managed-identity stack in ~20 minutes.
body:
A ready-to-present deck outline. Each slide has talking points and a one-line script you can read aloud. Move top to bottom and you will have taught the entire Foundry + MCP + managed-identity stack in ~20 minutes.

### The problem

- AI apps need a model **plus** identity, governance, tools, and observability
- Wiring each tool to each model by hand is an N×M mess
- Secrets sprawl is the #1 way these systems get breached

> We want an AI agent that can act on our real systems — safely, with no secrets, and without bespoke glue for every integration.

### Azure AI Foundry

- A governed Azure platform for building and operating AI apps
- Hierarchy: subscription → resource group → Foundry resource → project
- Portal for humans, SDK/CLI/REST for automation

> Foundry is the aircraft carrier: models are the aircraft, projects are the mission bays, and Azure governance is the command structure.

### Deploy & inference

- Pick a model from the catalog and create a **deployment** (an alias)
- Call chat completions through the project client
- Tokens in, tokens out; watch context window and usage

> A deployment is a named phone line to a model; we dial the deployment name, not the raw model version.

### Managed identity (the core)

- The workload gets an Entra identity with **no password**
- `DefaultAzureCredential` uses az login locally, managed identity in Azure
- RBAC grants least-privilege access per resource

> Managed identity is a building badge the platform issues and rotates for us — we never carry a key, and the badge only opens the doors RBAC allows.

### MCP in one breath

- MCP is the USB-C port for AI tools
- Host embeds Clients; each Client talks to one Server over JSON-RPC
- Servers expose Tools (actions), Resources (data), Prompts (templates)

> MCP standardizes how a model discovers and calls tools, so any compliant server plugs into any compliant host.

### Agents + MCP

- An agent = model + instructions + tools + state (threads/runs)
- Attach MCP servers as tools with a label, URL, and allowed tool list
- The agent carries the managed-identity token on every tool call

> The agent is the on-call engineer; MCP servers are its tools; managed identity is the badge it taps to use each one.

### ICM · Kusto · ADO

- **ICM**: query/ack/create incidents (writes need approval)
- **Kusto/ADX**: run KQL over telemetry — read-only mindset
- **ADO**: work items via WIQL, pipelines, repos/PRs

> Same pattern three times: an MCP server fronts the system, the agent calls tools, and a managed-identity token authorizes each call.

### Safety & observability

- Least privilege + human-in-the-loop on writes + full audit
- OpenTelemetry traces every call, tool, and token
- Preview features: verify against current Microsoft docs

> We let reads flow, gate writes behind approval, and trace everything — so the agent is powerful but never reckless.

drills.q: Slide "The problem": give the one-line script from memory.
drills.a: We want an AI agent that can act on our real systems — safely, with no secrets, and without bespoke glue for every integration.

drills.q: Slide "Azure AI Foundry": give the one-line script from memory.
drills.a: Foundry is the aircraft carrier: models are the aircraft, projects are the mission bays, and Azure governance is the command structure.

drills.q: Slide "Deploy & inference": give the one-line script from memory.
drills.a: A deployment is a named phone line to a model; we dial the deployment name, not the raw model version.

drills.q: Slide "Managed identity (the core)": give the one-line script from memory.
drills.a: Managed identity is a building badge the platform issues and rotates for us — we never carry a key, and the badge only opens the doors RBAC allows.

drills.q: Slide "MCP in one breath": give the one-line script from memory.
drills.a: MCP standardizes how a model discovers and calls tools, so any compliant server plugs into any compliant host.

drills.q: Slide "Agents + MCP": give the one-line script from memory.
drills.a: The agent is the on-call engineer; MCP servers are its tools; managed identity is the badge it taps to use each one.

drills.q: Slide "ICM · Kusto · ADO": give the one-line script from memory.
drills.a: Same pattern three times: an MCP server fronts the system, the agent calls tools, and a managed-identity token authorizes each call.

drills.q: Slide "Safety & observability": give the one-line script from memory.
drills.a: We let reads flow, gate writes behind approval, and trace everything — so the agent is powerful but never reckless.

teachItBack: Foundry is the governed platform, managed identity is the keyless badge, and MCP is the universal tool port. Present those three ideas first and every slide hangs off them.
